name: google-cloud-storage
description: Browse buckets and read, upload, and delete objects in Google Cloud Storage

tools.name: List Buckets
tools.description: List Cloud Storage buckets in a Google Cloud project

projectId.description: Google Cloud project ID that owns the buckets

prefix.description: Only return buckets whose names begin with this prefix

maxResults.description: Maximum buckets to return per page

pageToken.description: Continuation token from a previous page (nextPageToken)

tools.name: Get Bucket Metadata
tools.description: Get a bucket's metadata - location, storage class, versioning, and lifecycle configuration

projection.description: Set of properties to return: noAcl (default) or full

tools.name: List Objects
tools.description: List objects in a bucket, optionally filtered by name prefix or grouped by delimiter

prefix.description: Only return objects whose names begin with this prefix, e.g. 'reports/2026/'

delimiter.description: Group results like folders; use '/' to list one directory level at a time

maxResults.description: Maximum objects to return per page

pageToken.description: Continuation token from a previous page (nextPageToken)

tools.name: Download Object
tools.description: Download an object's content (alt=media), or set alt=json to return the object's metadata instead

objectName.description: Object name, URL-encoded — slashes in the name must be encoded as %2F (e.g. reports%2F2026%2Fsummary.csv)

alt.description: Response type: 'media' returns the object content, 'json' returns object metadata (size, contentType, updated, md5Hash, etc.)

tools.name: Upload Object
tools.description: Upload content as a new object (or overwrite an existing one) using a simple media upload — requires the read-write scope

uploadType.description: Upload protocol; 'media' sends the request body directly as the object content

name.description: Object name to create, including any folder-style prefix (e.g. reports/2026/summary.csv)

contentType.description: MIME type of the uploaded content

content.description: Raw object content sent as the request body

tools.name: Copy Object
tools.description: Copy an object to another name or bucket server-side (rewrite), without downloading and re-uploading - requires the read-write scope

sourceObject.description: Source object name, URL-encoded — slashes in the name must be encoded as %2F

destinationBucket.description: Bucket to copy the object into (may be the same as the source bucket)

destinationObject.description: Name for the new object, URL-encoded — slashes in the name must be encoded as %2F

rewriteToken.description: For very large objects: token from a previous rewrite response; repeat the call with it until the response reports done

tools.name: Delete Object
tools.description: Permanently delete an object from a bucket — requires the read-write scope

objectName.description: Object name to delete, URL-encoded — slashes in the name must be encoded as %2F

prompts.title: Browse a bucket
prompts.prompt: List the objects in one of my Cloud Storage buckets and summarize what's stored there.

prompts.title: Read a stored file
prompts.prompt: Download a file from my Cloud Storage bucket and summarize its contents.

prompts.title: Find stale objects
prompts.prompt: List objects in a bucket with their metadata and point out large or old files I might want to clean up.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Google Cloud Storage setup

steps.title: Create or select a Google Cloud project
steps.description: Go to https://console.cloud.google.com and create a project (new Google Cloud accounts include free-tier credit; Cloud Storage also has an always-free tier in some regions).

steps.title: Enable the Cloud Storage JSON API
steps.description: Open https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/library/storage-json.googleapis.com and click Enable for your project.

steps.title: Create OAuth credentials
steps.description: In https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials create an OAuth 2.0 Client ID (Web application) and add your callback URL as an authorized redirect URI. Store the values as GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET.

steps.title: Create a test bucket
steps.description: In https://console.cloud.google.com/storage/browser create a bucket and upload a small file so List Objects and Download Object have something to return.

notes: The default scope is read-only; uploading and deleting objects requires granting the optional devstorage.read_write scope during authorization.
List Buckets needs the project ID (not the project name or number label shown in the console header dropdown).
Object names that contain slashes must be URL-encoded (%2F) when used in the object path of download and delete calls.
